title: buildah: add support for `--retry` and `--retry-delay` incase of `push/pull` failures.
body:
Allows users to configure `--retry` attempts and `--retry-delay`
duration using two additional flags for commands

* buildah build
* buildah pull
* buildah push
* buildah from
* buildah add
* buildah copy

Closes: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/4018

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]
[NO TESTS NEEDED]
Not sure how we can test retry attempts in CI, but added a test in
tests/bud.bats which verifies we parse and added flag in other options
for sanity parsing checking.

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [flouthoc] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> Code LGTM but a test wouldn't hurt. At the very least to make sure the flags are parsed.

Added a test in tests/bud.bats which verifies we parse and in other options just passing flag with valid values to verify top level parsing does not fails.
@rhatdan Could you manually merge, cause rebase is not actually needed but gh would block. Otherwise i'll rebase and push again.
Nevermind I rebased just to be sure.

The `buildah add`, `buildah copy`, `buildah from`, and `buildah pull` commands don't push images, so their man pages shouldn't imply that they might.  Likewise, `buildah push` doesn't pull images.  The commit log lists `buildah push` twice in the list of commands it affects.
